description: 30 known dangerous drug interactions for evaluation. Each pair should be flagged as 'dangerous' or 'critical' by the CDSS.

interactions.risk: Increased bleeding risk (GI hemorrhage, intracranial bleeding)
Fatal pancytopenia — trimethoprim inhibits renal clearance of methotrexate
QT prolongation, torsades de pointes, sudden cardiac death
Rhabdomyolysis — CYP3A4 inhibition increases statin levels 10-20x
Lithium toxicity — NSAIDs reduce renal lithium clearance by 25%
Digoxin toxicity (nausea, arrhythmias) — amiodarone increases digoxin levels 70-100%
Serotonin syndrome — potentially fatal hyperthermia, rigidity, seizures
Fatal colchicine toxicity — CYP3A4 and P-gp inhibition
Lactic acidosis — contrast-induced AKI impairs metformin clearance
Reduced antiplatelet efficacy — CYP2C19 inhibition blocks clopidogrel activation
Severe hypotension, cardiovascular collapse — nitrate + PDE5i contraindicated
Life-threatening hyperkalemia, cardiac arrest
Loss of antifungal efficacy + potential carbamazepine toxicity
Severe hypotension, somnolence — CYP1A2 inhibition increases tizanidine AUC 10x
Serotonin syndrome or hypertensive crisis — MAOI + opioid contraindicated
QT prolongation, increased risk of cardiac arrhythmias
Ergotism (vasospasm, gangrene) — CYP3A4 inhibition
Hyperkalemia, hypotension, renal failure — dual RAAS blockade
Contraceptive failure — rifampicin induces CYP3A4 reducing ethinylestradiol levels
Theophylline toxicity (seizures, arrhythmias) — CYP1A2 inhibition
Major bleeding — P-gp inhibition increases dabigatran exposure 2-3x
Serotonin syndrome — linezolid is a weak MAOI
Antagonism of antiparkinson effect — dopamine receptor blockade
Cardiovascular collapse, hyperkalemia
Rhabdomyolysis — cyclosporine inhibits statin metabolism
Additive bone marrow suppression — agranulocytosis risk
Reduced tamoxifen efficacy — CYP2D6 inhibition blocks endoxifen formation
Myopathy/rhabdomyolysis — simvastatin dose must not exceed 20mg with amiodarone
Excessive uterine bleeding if timing/dosing not managed (used therapeutically but requires medical supervision)
QT prolongation, fatal arrhythmias — CYP3A4 inhibition raises pimozide levels
